evidence.text: Overall, I am encouraged by the trajectory; he is clinically stable and functionally improving, with no further events since July 2019.
two brief events on 14/09 and three overnight on 27/09, then a quiet week).

pipeline.raw_value: multiple cluster per week, 2 to 3 per cluster
pipeline.normalized_value: multiple cluster per week, 2 to 3 per cluster

evidence.text: Over the past six weeks he has noticed that brief episodes are tending to bunch together after he wakes, occurring on several mornings each week despite taking his tablets as prescribed. He describes these as short spells of disorientation with a metallic taste and a need to pause for 30–60 seconds, without loss of awareness, sometimes repeating two or three times within the same morning.

deterministic_step.label: normalization bridge
deterministic_step.output: multiple cluster per week, 2 to 3 per cluster

evidence.text: The patient reports being free of events for the past seven months, with no witnessed episodes, auras, or nocturnal disturbances reported by the household.
In terms of seizure control, She had no seizures for nearly a year following initiation of Valproate, then developed myoclonic jerks leading to a tonic seizure three Saturdays ago
Electrographic seizures frequent on EEG (~4/h) were recorded during a recent ambulatory study, correlating with subtle clinical pauses observed by the technician and by his partner on diary review.

pipeline.raw_value: 4 cluster per 3 month, multiple per cluster
pipeline.normalized_value: 4 cluster per 3 month, multiple per cluster

evidence.text: Seizure frequency: the family have maintained a detailed diary and confirm 4 clusters this quarter, each brief and self-limiting

deterministic_step.label: normalization bridge
deterministic_step.output: 4 cluster per 3 month, multiple per cluster

evidence.text: She reports nocturnal episodes occurring once per night on average for the past three months, typically between 02:00 and 04:00.
Over the past two months she reports six drop attacks and two absence seizures in the past two months, typically occurring in the late afternoon.
No further tonic-clonic seizures have occurred since 12/2020, although two to three single jerks remain, typically noted at the end of long shifts or on waking after reduced sleep.
On today’s assessment, she remains entirely seizure-free in adult life; in practical terms, she has had uninterrupted stability without any seizure occurrence.
Patient reports 2 clusters this month; each ≈4 absences in the morning.
They have kept a shared digital log over the past month showing events occurring q1 - 2d, typically in the late afternoon, with occasional clustering around periods of stress or poor sleep.
Considering that he has now been free of seizures for three weeks and his overall condition is stable, no immediate action is necessary.

The patient now reports brief focal impaired-awareness seizures occurring every 2 days, often with a rising epigastric sensation, behavioural arrest, lip smacking and subsequent confusion lasting 10–15 minutes.
Electrographic seizures frequent on EEG (~6/h).
Over the last eight weeks, simple partial seizures happen after lack of sleep, typically one to two events in the morning following nights with less than five hours of rest.

Seizure summary: Seizure frequency has not altered significantly over the past six months; he continues to suffer one to two generalised tonic-clonic seizures per month, with a longest seizure-free interval of around three weeks.
Since her last review she has kept a clear diary and reports five focal sensory and six focal non-motors in last month, with several occurring after nights of reduced sleep.

Generalised epilepsy with nocturnal and occasional daytime events; good response to Sodium Valproate, partial response to Levetiracetam previously; Lamotrigine avoided due to patient-reported mood effects.
She reports one absence seizure every one to two day and has been free of tonic-clonic seizures for over nine months.
She had 7 convulsions so far in Sep, 4 in Aug, 2 in Jul, one in Jun

Seizure history since the last review: In November he had a run of one seizure within half an hour (not full generalised tonic-clonic, fluctuating in intensity, resolved without medication). In December there was 7 further seizures at night, and in March another during physiotherapy (unwitnessed by mother). He associates the November and December events with multiple red-eye flights, jet lag and fragmented sleep. The March episode followed a strenuous rehabilitation session for a minor ankle injury while he was mildly sleep-deprived; there was no reported trauma, tongue biting or incontinence.

He had a cluster of three seizures in August (short, not full convulsions, fluctuating awareness, self-terminating). In October he had six nocturnal seizures, and in January a single tonic seizure was recorded during respite care.
Following introduction of lamotrigine, there has been a clear improvement in control, with just seven generalised tonic-clonic seizures documented this year to date

pipeline.raw_value: 2 cluster per 3 month, multiple per cluster
pipeline.normalized_value: 2 cluster per 3 month, multiple per cluster

Seizure History This Quarter:

Patient reports two clusters this quarter, both occurring in the context of poor sleep and intercurrent upper respiratory symptoms

evidence.text: Following introduction of lamotrigine, there has been a clear improvement in control, with just five generalised tonic-clonic seizures documented this year to date
Cluster burden increased since 1 January 2024; now weekly, two per cluster.
He keeps a diary and, based on his report: In Oct he had no nocturnal seizures but 5 daytime events. In December he had two nocturnal seizures and two while awake.

Over the past three weeks he has been experiencing seizures twice a day, typically one in the early morning between 05:00–07:00 and another in the late evening, sometimes preceded by brief tingling in the left cheek.

Seizure control is inconsistent and while the caregiver perceives stability, she continues to have nightly generalised convulsions seizures and intermittent tonic seizures 2 times per year, this has been her enduring seizure profile for many years.
Seizures: The patient reports brief episodes of loss of awareness occurring abs *monthly, typically in the late evening, each lasting under one minute with rapid recovery and no clear precipitant.
Over the last several weeks she has noticed that episodes are now tending to occur at most on a monthly basis, and some months have passed without any events at all.
He reports episodes consistent with focal or generalised onset being unclear at present, with a current frequency of ≤ 3 per day on days with prolonged screen exposure and irregular sleep.
She noted a seizure in Sep, 2 in Aug, and four in Jul, all from mixed awake/asleep states.
He reports "sz *three/d" on average during evening shifts, more commonly when moving between the grill and fry station in a high-heat, fast-paced environment.

He previously suffered from frequent clusters of myoclonic jerks, absence seizures and occasional convulsive events 1 or 2 times per year, but is currently in long-term remission, having been seizure free for years
No convulsive events reported in the past year.
Recurrent unprovoked seizures, current pattern worsening with medication adherence difficulties in a secure cleanroom/data‑hall workplace; Occasional prolonged seizure event up to 12 minutes.
The patient reports that day-to-day functioning has stabilised and, importantly, there have been effectively no spell-like events suggestive of seizures over the past six months.
Focal epilepsy with sleep deprivation and circadian disruption as prominent precipitants; reports seizure events during travel/jet lag
The calendar entries from 01 June 2025 to 30 September 2025 document consistent daily logs without gaps.
